CalcParams.description:
Defines the parameters for calculating the cost-optimized Digital Twin deployment.

Server-side validation ensures:
- Positive values for device counts, intervals, and sizes
- Storage duration ordering: Hot ≤ Cool ≤ Archive
- Non-negative values for editor/viewer counts and dashboard settings

allowGcpSelfHostedL4.description: Include GCP self-hosted L4 (Twin Management on Compute Engine) in optimization - NOT IMPLEMENTED
allowGcpSelfHostedL4.title: Allowgcpselfhostedl4

allowGcpSelfHostedL5.description: Include GCP self-hosted L5 (Grafana on Compute Engine) in optimization - NOT IMPLEMENTED
allowGcpSelfHostedL5.title: Allowgcpselfhostedl5

averageDigitalTwinQueryResponseSizeInKb.description: Estimated average Azure Digital Twins query response size in KB
averageDigitalTwinQueryResponseSizeInKb.title: Averagedigitaltwinqueryresponsesizeinkb

averageDigitalTwinQueryUnitsPerQuery.description: Estimated average Azure Digital Twins query units per logical query
averageDigitalTwinQueryUnitsPerQuery.title: Averagedigitaltwinqueryunitsperquery

averageSizeOfMessageInKb.description: Average message size in KB (must be > 0)
averageSizeOfMessageInKb.title: Averagesizeofmessageinkb

eventTriggerRate.description: Fraction of messages that trigger events (0.0-1.0)
eventTriggerRate.title: Eventtriggerrate

numberOfDeviceTypes.description: Number of distinct device types (each requires a processor)
numberOfDeviceTypes.title: Numberofdevicetypes

numberOfEventActions.description: Number of event action handlers from config_events.json
numberOfEventActions.title: Numberofeventactions

optimizationProfileId.description: Executable optimization profile. Only cost_minimization_v1 is enabled.
optimizationProfileId.title: Optimizationprofileid

providerPricingCatalogs.description: Exact reviewed AWS, Azure, and GCP provider-region catalog references. Calculations never resolve a mutable latest snapshot.

providerPricingContexts.description: Management-injected provider account pricing observations. Clients cannot infer an AWS TwinMaker plan.

CalcParams.title: CalcParams

ErrorResponse.description:
Standardized error response with detailed information for troubleshooting.

This schema provides structured error information that enables:
1. Understanding what went wrong (error_code, message)
2. Knowing exactly how to fix it (fix_suggestion)
3. Identifying specific field issues (field_errors)
4. Finding additional help (documentation_url)

All error responses from this API follow this schema for consistency.

field_errors.message: Value must be a positive integer

example.fix_suggestion: Ensure all required fields are present and have valid values. See field_errors for details.
example.message: Request validation failed

documentation_url.description: URL to relevant documentation for this error or endpoint
documentation_url.title: Documentation Url

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE format. Use this to programmatically identify error types. Common codes: VALIDATION_ERROR, INVALID_CREDENTIALS, RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND, RATE_LIMITED, INTERNAL_ERROR, CONFIGURATION_MISSING
error_code.title: Error Code

field_errors.description: For validation errors (422), detailed list of which fields failed and why
field_errors.title: Field Errors

fix_suggestion.description: Actionable instruction that explains exactly how to fix this error and retry the request successfully. Be specific about what to change.
fix_suggestion.example: Set 'numberOfDevices' to a positive integer (e.g., 100). This field represents the number of IoT devices in your deployment.
fix_suggestion.title: Fix Suggestion

http_status.description: The HTTP status code associated with this error (400, 401, 403, 404, 422, 500, etc.)
http_status.title: Http Status

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong
message.example: Request validation failed: numberOfDevices must be positive
message.title: Message

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse

FieldError.description:
Describes a validation error for a specific request field.

Use this to understand exactly which field failed validation
and what values would be acceptable.

example.message: Invalid cloud provider specified

allowed_values.description: If applicable, the list of valid values for this field
allowed_values.title: Allowed Values

field.description: The name of the field that failed validation (dot notation for nested fields)
field.title: Field

message.description: Human-readable explanation of why this field failed validation
message.example: Value must be a positive integer
message.title: Message

received_value.description: The actual value that was received (stringified for display)
received_value.title: Received Value

FieldError.title: FieldError

GCPCredentialsRequest.title: GCPCredentialsRequest

ValidationResponse.description:
Validation response with all errors found.

When valid=true, the configuration is complete and ready for deployment.
When valid=false, check the errors array for what needs to be fixed.

errors.message: Optimizer result required - run cost calculation in Step 2

errors.description: List of all validation errors (empty if valid=true)
errors.title: Errors

valid.description: True if configuration is complete and valid, False if there are errors
valid.title: Valid

ValidationResponse.title: ValidationResponse

info.description:
API backend for **Twin2Clouds**, a cost-optimization platform for engineering digital twins across multiple cloud providers (AWS, Azure, Google). This API serves both the web UI and the computational engine that calculates the most cost-efficient provider setup for each architectural layer.

🔗 Useful Links

🖥️ Web Interface

  • Open Web UI
    The graphical Twin2Clouds interface for configuring scenarios.

📘 Documentation

  • Documentation Overview
info.title: twin2clouds REST API

put.description:
**Purpose:** Computes the most cost-effective distribution of Digital Twin services across AWS, Azure, and GCP based on your scenario parameters and current cloud pricing.

**When to use this endpoint:**
- Before deploying a new Digital Twin to determine the cheapest provider configuration
- When comparing costs across different scenario configurations
- To understand cost breakdown by architectural layer

**How it works:**
1. Takes your Digital Twin parameters (device count, message frequency, storage needs, etc.)
2. Resolves the exact reviewed provider-region catalogs supplied in `providerPricingCatalogs`
3. Calculates costs for each of the 5 architectural layers on each provider
4. Returns the optimal provider per layer, detailed cost breakdowns, and the same catalog references

**The 5 Architectural Layers:**
- **L1 (Ingestion):** IoT data acquisition - receives telemetry from devices
- **L2 (Processing):** Data processing, event detection, notifications
- **L3 (Storage):** Hot/Cool/Archive storage tiers - each can be on different providers
- **L4 (Management):** Digital Twin entity management and 3D modeling
- **L5 (Visualization):** Dashboards and user interfaces

**Important:** This is a calculation-only endpoint. It does not deploy any resources. Use the Deployer API's `/infrastructure/deploy` to actually provision infrastructure.

200.description: Successful calculation - returns cost breakdown and optimal configuration

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'
example.message: Missing required parameter: provider

400.description: Bad Request - The request was malformed or missing required parameters

example.fix_suggestion: Refresh the affected provider-region pricing catalog and retry
example.message: Pricing catalog snapshot is stale

409.description: Conflict - A required catalog or operation state is not usable

example.fix_suggestion: Check field_errors for specific issues and correct the values
example.message: Request validation failed

422.description: Validation Error - Request body failed schema validation

example.fix_suggestion: Retry the request. If the error persists, check server logs or contact support.
example.message: An unexpected error occurred while processing your request

500.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred

put.summary: Calculate optimal multi-cloud cost distribution for Digital Twin deployment

post.description:
**Purpose:** Fetches current AWS pricing data and caches it locally for use in cost calculations.

**When to use:**
- Before running calculations if pricing data is stale (>7 days old)
- Debug/local-cloud mode when you need guaranteed fresh pricing data from mounted credential files
- After AWS announces pricing changes

**Behavior:**
- Uses cached data if less than 7 days old (unless force_fetch=true)
- Fresh file-based refresh requires `ENABLE_LOCAL_CREDENTIAL_FILE_CHECKS=true`
- App flows should use `/fetch_pricing_with_credentials/aws`
- May take 30-60 seconds for a full refresh

example.fix_suggestion: Verify your credentials in config_credentials.json or provide valid credentials in the request body
example.message: Cloud provider credentials are invalid or expired

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication credentials are missing or invalid

example.fix_suggestion: Use request-body credential validation or start the explicit local-cloud Compose override.
example.message: File-based credential checks are disabled for this runtime.

403.description: Forbidden - This runtime mode does not allow the requested operation

example.fix_suggestion: Retry the request. If the error persists, check server logs or contact support.
example.message: An unexpected error occurred while processing your request

500.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred

post.description:
**Purpose:** Fetches current Azure pricing data and caches it locally.

**When to use:**
- Before running calculations if pricing data is stale
- The Azure Retail Prices API is public - no credentials required

**Behavior:**
- Uses cached data if less than 7 days old (unless force_fetch=true)
- No authentication required (public API)

post.description:
**Purpose:** Fetches current Google Cloud pricing data and caches it locally.

**When to use:**
- Before running calculations if pricing data is stale (>7 days old)
- Debug/local-cloud mode when mounted GCP service account credentials are available

**Behavior:**
- Uses cached data if less than 7 days old (unless force_fetch=true)
- Fresh file-based refresh requires `ENABLE_LOCAL_CREDENTIAL_FILE_CHECKS=true`
- App flows should use `/fetch_pricing_with_credentials/gcp`

**When to use:**

get.description:
**Purpose:** Check if AWS pricing data is fresh and valid before running calculations.

**Use this to decide:** Should I call `refreshAwsPricing` before `calculateOptimalCloudDistribution`?

**Response fields:**
- `is_fresh`: true if data aged < 7 days
- `status`: 'valid', 'incomplete', 'missing', or 'error'
- `missing_keys`: service keys that are missing if incomplete
get.summary: Check age and validity of cached AWS pricing data

get.description:
**Purpose:** Check if Azure pricing data is fresh and valid before running calculations.

**Use this to decide:** Should I call `refreshAzurePricing` before `calculateOptimalCloudDistribution`?
get.summary: Check age and validity of cached Azure pricing data

get.description:
**Purpose:** Check if GCP pricing data is fresh and valid before running calculations.

**Use this to decide:** Should I call `refreshGcpPricing` before `calculateOptimalCloudDistribution`?
get.summary: Check age and validity of cached GCP pricing data

get.description: Returns the age and freshness of the local GCP regions cache (30-day threshold).

post.description:
**Purpose:** Stream real-time logs during pricing data fetch via Server-Sent Events.

**Event types:**
- `log`: Progress message from pricing fetch operations
- `heartbeat`: Keep-alive signal (every 10s during long operations)
- `complete`: Pricing fetch completed successfully
- `error`: An error occurred during fetch

**Dual Output:** Logs appear in both SSE stream AND container logs.

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'
example.message: Missing required parameter: provider

400.description: Bad Request - The request was malformed or missing required parameters

post.summary: SSE stream for pricing fetch with real-time logs

post.description:
**Purpose:** Validates that the optimizer configuration is complete and ready for state transition to the deployment phase.

**When to use:**
- Before transitioning from 'optimized' to 'ready_to_deploy' state
- To verify user has completed all Step 2 configuration

**Validation checks:**
- `params`: Optimizer parameters must be present (device count, intervals, etc.)
- `result`: Calculation result must be present (run the calculation first)
- `result.cheapestPath`: The optimal provider path must be calculated

**Returns:** `valid=true` if ready, or list of errors detailing what's missing.

example.fix_suggestion: Check field_errors for specific issues and correct the values
example.message: Request validation failed

422.description: Validation Error - Request body failed schema validation
